summary.intent: Set up automated Sentry issue remediation with three handoff modes: immediate MCP-based fixes, CLI script for local/CI use, and webhook-based assignment routing that triggers Codex or Claude when Sentry issues are assigned to them.
summary.outcome: Built all three modes: sentry_to_codex.sh CLI script (validates, creates PRs per issue), updated GitHub Actions workflow with Codex integration, and implemented Sentry webhook endpoint with assignee-based routing to Codex/Claude. All code compiles and tests pass (9/9 sentry routing tests green).

repo: The remediation pipeline was mostly built (sentry_findings.py, remediation_runner.py, GH Actions workflow) but missing the Codex CLI invocation and webhook receiver
Policy guardrails already exist in .harness/policy.contract.json (path restrictions, max files/lines, phase rollout)
Ollama is configured as a Homebrew service and auto-starts on login via ~/Library/LaunchAgents/homebrew.mxcl.ollama.plist
The contextplus MCP server auto-starts from .mcp.json when sessions begin, providing semantic_code_search and other tools via Ollama (nomic-embed-text + llama3.2)
Sentry webhook payloads have multiple assignedTo formats: object with name/slug, string shorthand 'team:codex', and data.assignee fallback

code.finding: Agent loop never calls touch_active() during execution, causing heartbeat monitor to falsely flag agents as unresponsive during long LLM calls or tool execution
LLM retry path (call_with_retry) has no provider fallback - after 3 retries on rate limits, it fails and logs to Sentry. 5.4K errors in 24h suggests Groq rate limits are hit consistently
Sentry webhook handler needs to parse assignedTo from multiple formats (issue.assignedTo.name, issue.assignedTo.slug, data.assignee string) to route to Codex/Claude correctly

workflow: GitHub Actions workflows can read repo variables via vars.VARNAME and secrets via secrets.VARNAME
Sentry internal integrations provide webhook signing secrets (Client Secret) but require separate auth tokens for API access (member:write scope)
Codex CLI invocation pattern: codex exec --json --approval-mode full-auto with system prompt and validation commands
One PR per Sentry issue (not batched) makes reviews/reverts cleaner
Branch naming strategy codex/sentry-fix-{short_id} prevents duplicate remediation runs

friction: Mode 1 (live Codex MCP fix) was rejected by user, blocking immediate remediation
Spent 3.5 hours in brainstorming/planning overhead before building actual code
Background agent for webhook endpoint was slow (long compile time), had to kill and finish manually
Disk full error (62GB in target/) blocked test compilation until cleanup
Sentry auth token vs webhook secret confusion - webhook secret can't be used for API calls like member invites
Email-based assignee invites failed, had to fall back to team-based assignment
open_items: Fix agent_loop.rs to add touch_active() calls during long operations to prevent false 'unresponsive' warnings
Add provider fallback logic in LLM retry path to handle Groq rate limits (5.4K errors/day, 58% failure rate)
Set SENTRY_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var on production OpenFang server
Replace placeholder webhook URL https://openfang.example.com/api/webhooks/sentry with actual hostname
Set GitHub secrets/vars: OPENAI_API_KEY, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN, SENTRY_ORG_SLUG, SENTRY_PROJECT_SLUG
Run chmod +x on scripts/sentry_to_codex.sh (blocked by sandbox)
Test end-to-end flow: assign Sentry issue to #codex team and verify webhook triggers remediation
